segments.text: When Israel was a child, then I loved him, and called my son out of Egypt.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text:
They called to them, so they went from them. They sacrificed to the Baals, 
and burned incense to engraved images.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
Yet I taught Ephraim to walk. I took them by their arms, 
but they didn’t know that I healed them.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text:
I drew them with cords of a man, with ties of love; and I was to them like those who lift up the yoke on their necks; 
and I bent down to him and I fed him.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text:
“They won’t return into the land of Egypt; but the Assyrian will be their king, 
because they refused to repent.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text:
The sword will fall on their cities, and will destroy the bars of their gates, 
and will put an end to their plans.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text:
My people are determined to turn from me. Though they call to the Most High, 
he certainly won’t exalt them.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text:
“How can I give you up, Ephraim? How can I hand you over, Israel?
How can I make you like Admah?
How can I make you like Zeboiim? My heart is turned within me, my compassion is aroused.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text:
I will not execute the fierceness of my anger. I will not return to destroy Ephraim, 
for I am God, and not man—the Holy One among you. 
I will not come in wrath.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text:
They will walk after Yahweh, who will roar like a lion; 
for he will roar, and the children will come trembling from the west.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: They will come trembling like a bird out of Egypt, and like a dove out of the land of Assyria; and I will settle them in their houses,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Ephraim surrounds me with falsehood, and the house of Israel with deceit.

Judah still strays from God,
and is unfaithful to the Holy One.
